game_name: Ogre Battle: The March of the Black Queen

danger_signals.visual_form: Vampires appearing in coffins during day; werewolves appearing as normal men during day but transforming at night
danger_signals.meaning: Time-of-day danger indicator; certain threats only manifest under darkness

danger_signals.visual_form: Marked enemy unit representing stage commander
danger_signals.meaning: Primary objective threat; defeating boss completes liberation mission

danger_signals.visual_form: Previously captured town reverting to enemy control on map
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of territory and income if tactical position not maintained

danger_signals.visual_form: Opposing squad moving close to player unit on tactical map
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent engagement; battle will trigger when units are adjacent

safety_signals.visual_form: Town under player faction control on map
safety_signals.meaning: Generates income each day cycle; provides shop access and information

safety_signals.visual_form: Day phase on alternating day/night cycle
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced threat from nocturnal monsters (vampires, werewolves disabled)
Strategic position; can be held as defensive locations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy units and boss units appear as Zeteginean forces attempting to recapture towns and temples
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupied towns and temples; controlled military positions on stage map

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player-controlled units display under player squad control; distinct from enemy forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Liberated towns and temples; player-deployed squad positions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Time-of-day indicator showing daylight; nocturnal enemies (vampires in coffins, werewolves as normal men) rendered harmless
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vampires and werewolves unable to function; removed from active threat roster during daylight hours

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Time-of-day indicator showing darkness; vampires emerge from coffins, werewolves transform to combat form
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Nocturnal enemies become active combatants; increased danger threat; different tactical composition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Town/temple icon changes to player faction control on map; visual distinction from enemy-held locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Town begins generating income; becomes accessible for shopping and information; if undefended, will revert to Zeteginean control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Previously liberated town reverts to enemy control indicator on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lost income source; loss of access to shop and information; enemy presence reinstated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss unit removed from map; stage completion indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission objective complete; stage unlocked for future replay; progression to next stage enabled

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Reputation score displayed; affects character recruitment visuals and dialogue tone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High-alignment characters willing to join; certain story branches and recruits only available to high-reputation leader

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Reputation score displayed; affects character recruitment visuals and dialogue tone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Low-alignment (evil) characters willing to join; alternate recruits and story branches available only to low-reputation leader

environmental_storytelling.technique: Town liberation with random tarot card draw
environmental_storytelling.example: Upon capturing a town, a Major Arcana card is drawn, providing variable effects to reflect fortune/consequence of liberation; creates sense of consequence and unpredictability
Some towns and temples do not display on initial map; only revealed when unit moves over location; rewards exploration and encourages thorough tactical positioning
Vampire visual form changes from coffin (day) to active combatant (night); werewolf appears as normal man (day) vs. beast form (night); communicates danger differently by time period
Deploying and maintaining squads costs money each day cycle; reinforces that liberated towns must generate income to sustain revolution; creates economic strategy layer
13 different endings based on reputation and moral choices; game visually/narratively reflects player alignment through story branches and character availability

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Renders in coffin form; non-functional; cannot engage in combat
Emerges from coffin; active combatant; functional threat unit
Appears as normal man; non-threatening appearance; non-functional as combat unit
Transforms to beast form; active combatant; functional threat unit
Battle engagement triggered; unit moves to close-up battle view
Defeated unit retreats from map; victor unit remains in tactical position
Town captured by Zeteginean forces; reverts to enemy control; income generation stops
Removed from map; stage completion achieved; progression enabled

notes: Ogre Battle uses a sophisticated real-time tactical system where world state is communicated through time-of-day cycles (affecting enemy manifestation), territorial control markers (town/temple occupation), and alignment systems that determine available characters and story branches. The game employs visual transformation (vampires/werewolves) and icon-based map control to convey danger and political control. Tarot cards function as both mechanical randomizers and narrative devices, adding unpredictability to world state changes. The 13-ending structure ensures that player conduct visually and narratively manifests in endgame state.
